question:
The ANOVA in Exercises 13.3 found that birth weight of four groups of infants differed significantly according to the smoking status of mothers ( $P=$ 0.042 ). Table 13.3 lists the data for the analysis. Data are also available online in the file SMOK_BW.SAV.
(a) Conduct post hoc comparisons using the LSD method. Summarize these results in concise narrative form.
(b) Incorporate Bonferroni's correction into each of the $P$-value calculated in part (a).
(c) Calculate $95 \%$ confidence intervals for all possible mean differences incorporating a Bonferroni adjustment..
answer:
(a) Least squared difference (LSD) tests

 egin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Null hypothesis & $ ar{x}_i- ar{x}_j$ & SE & $P$-value (2-sided) \
\hline $H_0: \mu_1-\mu_2=0$ & 0.0857 & 0.67848 & 0.901 \
\hline $H_0: \mu_1-\mu_3=0$ & 1.1191 & 0.64690 & 0.098 \
\hline $H_0: \mu_1-\mu_4=0$ & 1.6129 & 0.60512 & 0.014 \
\hline $H_0: \mu_2-\mu_3=0$ & 1.0334 & 0.67848 & 0.143 \
\hline $H_0: \mu_2-\mu_4=0$ & 1.5272 & 0.63876 & 0.026 \
\hline $H_0: \mu_3-\mu_4=0$ & 0.4938 & 0.60512 & 0.424 \
\hline
\end{tabular}

Calculated by SPSS (Rel. 11.0.1. 2001. Chicago: SPSS Inc.)

Summary in concise narrative form: Differences between group 1 and group 4 and between group 2 and group 4 are statistically significant. The difference between group 1 and group 3 is marginally significant.
(b) Bonferroni's method tests

 egin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Null hypothesis & $ ar{x}_i- ar{x}_j$ & SE & $P$-value (2-sided) \
\hline $H_0: \mu_1-\mu_2=0$ & 0.0857 & 0.67848 & 1.000 \
\hline $H_0: \mu_1-\mu_3=0$ & 1.1191 & 0.64690 & 0.590 \
\hline $H_0: \mu_1-\mu_4=0$ & 1.6129 & 0.60512 & 0.087 \
\hline $H_0: \mu_2-\mu_3=0$ & 1.0334 & 0.67848 & 0.856 \
\hline $H_0: \mu_2-\mu_4=0$ & 1.5272 & 0.63876 & 0.158 \
\hline $H_0: \mu_3-\mu_4=0$ & 0.4938 & 0.60512 & 1.000 \
\hline
\end{tabular}

Calculated with SPSS (Rel. 11.0.1. 2001. Chicago: SPSS Inc.)

Summary in concise narrative form: The difference between group 1 and group 4 is marginally significant. All other differences are not statistically significant.
(c) Post hoc confidence intervals incorporating Bonferroni's correction

 egin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline \multirow{2}{*}{Parameter} & \multirow[b]{2}{*}{$ ar{x}_i- ar{x}_j$} & \multirow[b]{2}{*}{SE} & \multicolumn{2}{|c|}{95\% confidence interval} \
\hline & & & LCL & UCL \
\hline $\mu_1-\mu_2$ & 0.0857 & 0.67848 & -1.8901 & 2.0615 \
\hline $\mu_1-\mu_3$ & 1.1191 & 0.64690 & -0.7647 & 3.0030 \
\hline $\mu_1-\mu_4$ & 1.6129 & 0.60512 & -0.1492 & 3.3751 \
\hline $\mu_2-\mu_3$ & 1.0334 & 0.67848 & -0.9424 & 3.0092 \
\hline $\mu_2-\mu_4$ & 1.5272 & 0.63876 & -0.3329 & 3.3873 \
\hline $\mu_3-\mu_4$ & 0.4938 & 0.60512 & -1.2684 & 2.2559 \
\hline
\end{tabular}

Calculated with SPSS (Rel. 11.0.1. 2001. Chicago: SPSS Inc.)
